graph.name: Global Multi-Region Ecommerce - 120k RPS Architecture Test

evaluations.label: Average traffic
evaluations.reason: The design sustains an estimated 150044.99 events/s, above the 120000 events/s requirement.

evaluations.label: Peak traffic
evaluations.reason: The design sustains an estimated 150044.99 events/s, below the 360000 events/s requirement.

evaluations.label: Average latency
evaluations.reason: Measured 220 ms stays within the 280 ms limit.

evaluations.label: p95 latency
evaluations.reason: Measured 410 ms stays within the 750 ms limit.

evaluations.label: Data loss
evaluations.reason: Measured 0 events stays within the 0 events limit.

evaluations.label: Recovery time
evaluations.reason: The simulator does not yet measure recovery time. Keep this goal as an open question until failure scenarios are simulated over time.

evaluations.label: Recovery point
evaluations.reason: The simulator does not yet measure how much work is lost during recovery. Keep this goal as an open question until failure scenarios are simulated over time.

evaluations.label: Data staleness
evaluations.reason: Data freshness is not yet modeled. Keep this goal as an open question until read paths declare their staleness behavior.

evaluations.label: Ordering
evaluations.reason: The per-key ordering requirement can be checked once every edge declares its delivery policy.

assumptions: Sustainable traffic is extrapolated linearly from the tightest component in the simulated scenario.
End-to-end availability is estimated as the product of node availabilities; failover paths are not credited.

userImpact.description: Redelivered by retries; consumers must tolerate seeing the same work twice.

confidenceReasons: Observed metrics are available for calibration.
assumptions: Traffic and failures are deterministic for the same graph and profile.
Node capacity is estimated from configured limits.
Infrastructure clients are not executed by the simulator.
Reported latency covers the caller-facing synchronous path; caller latency ends at asynchronous boundaries.
Timeline values are estimates integrated in 15s frames; node metrics are whole-scenario averages.
